data.title: UK's Ten Lifestyle interim net revenue rises on member growth
data.description:
Overview

  • UK customer loyalty platform's interim net revenue grew 6% yr/yr to £33.7 mln

  • Adjusted EBITDA rose 16% yr/yr, reflecting operational leverage and digital transformation

  • Active members increased 23% yr/yr, driven by higher digital platform engagement

Outlook

  • Ten Lifestyle expects FY 2026 trading in line with market expectations

  • Company says it expects FY 2027 revenue and adj EBITDA to be ahead of current market forecasts

  • Board says macro and geopolitical risks, including Middle East conflict, have had limited impact

Result Drivers

  • DIGITAL TRANSFORMATION - Co said ongoing investment in digital platform and AI systems improved efficiency, scalability, and service quality

  • MEMBER GROWTH - 23% increase in active members attributed to higher engagement with digital platform

  • CONTRACT MOMENTUM - New and expanded digital contracts with financial institutions and premium brands supported revenue growth

Company press release:

  • The average consensus recommendation for the leisure & recreation peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Ten Lifestyle Group PLC is GBp130.00, about 75.7% above its April 21 closing price of GBp74.00

data.title: Sweden's Corem Property Q1 income hit by lower occupancy
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden real estate firm's Q1 income and net operating income declined yr/yr amid lower occupancy

  • Company reported negative net letting for Q1, reflecting cautious demand and recent tenant terminations

  • Company continued share repurchases and finalized sale of US property, boosting liquidity for Q2

Outlook

  • Company expects higher interest rates in 2026, which may increase interest expense

  • Corem anticipates increased leasing activity and a more dynamic second quarter

  • Company expects significant liquidity inflow in Q2 from 417 Park Avenue sale, boosting financial flexibility

Result Drivers

  • TENANT TERMINATIONS - Lower occupancy and revenue due to large tenant terminations, including Ericsson's exit in Kista, per company

  • HIGHER OPERATING COSTS - Cold winter led to increased heating and snow removal expenses, impacting costs, per company

  • CAUTIOUS MARKET DEMAND - Negative net letting reflects cautious demand and longer decision cycles among tenants, per company

Company press release:

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Corem Property Group AB is SEK4.70, about 14.4% above its December 12 closing price of SEK4.11

data.title: UK's Aberdeen posts nearly $4 billion of outflows for first quarter
data.description:
British money manager Aberdeen on Wednesday posted net outflows of 2.9 billion pounds ($3.92 billion) for the first quarter, in-line with peers, as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East weighed on investor appetite and global markets.

Global markets have whipsawed since the Iran war began, with investor sentiment closely tied to developments in the conflict. Money managers such as Schroders and Ashmore last week said they saw heavy client withdrawals in March amid war‑driven volatility.

The London-based company said its managed assets fell to 547.7 billion pounds at the end of March, from the 556 billion pounds reported at December-end.

The firm said lower markets and previously announced equity withdrawals weighed on assets despite record growth at its interactive investor platform.

($1 = 0.7394 pounds)

data.title: Sweden's VNV Global NAV per share falls 12% in Q1
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden investment firm's Q1 NAV per share fell 15% in USD, 12% in SEK

  • Net result for Q1 was a loss, mainly from negative revaluations of BlaBlaCar and Voi

  • Company repurchased 487,358 common shares during the quarter

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific guidance or outlook for future periods

Result Drivers

  • PORTFOLIO REVALUATIONS - Co said negative revaluations of BlaBlaCar and Voi drove net loss

  • LOWER PEER MULTIPLES - Co attributed NAV decline to lower peer multiples across portfolio, citing ongoing conflict in Middle East and rapid AI developments

data.title: Bull Case for India Hospitality Sector Backed by Luxury, Corporate Travel Demand — Market Talk
data.description: The bull case for India's hospitality sector is backed by luxury and corporate travel demand, Nomura's Akash Gupta says in a research report. The sector is in a structural upcycle, given a widening demand-supply gap in luxury hospitality and resilient growth in corporate travel, the analyst says. This should sustain strong average-daily-rate-led growth of the sector. Nomura initiates coverage of Leela Palaces Hotels & Resorts with a buy rating and a target price of 510.00 rupees and Chalet Hotels with a neutral rating and target price of 860.00 rupees. Meanwhile, the brokerage lowers the target price of ITC Hotels' stock to 210.00 rupees from 230.00 rupees with an unchanged buy rating. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: South African Markets - Factors to watch on April 22
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events, equity and currency market moves may affect South African markets on Wednesday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

March consumer price index (ZACPIY=ECI)

February retail sales data (ZARET=ECI)

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

The South African rand weakened on Tuesday as investors weighed geopolitical tensions linked to the Iran war, while investors digested weaker domestic business confidence data and warnings from the central bank about inflation risks linked to the conflict.

On the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, the Top-40 index was down 1.47%.

GLOBAL MARKETS

U.S. stock futures rose and the dollar wavered on Wednesday after President Donald Trump said he would indefinitely extend the Iran ceasefire, keeping sentiment buoyed, although with the Strait of Hormuz still closed, oil prices stayed near $100.

GOLD

Gold prices firmed on Wednesday as lower oil prices, following a U.S. extension of a ceasefire with Iran, eased fears of an inflation spike and prolonged high interest rates.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

- - - -

Some of the main stories out in the South African press:

NEWS24

- Upcoming elections are funded, despite R1.6bn budget deficit, says IEC

- Draft forex rules ditch some apartheid-era restrictions

data.description:
